Title: Organic acid anions: An effective defensive weapon for plants against aluminum toxicity and phosphorus deficiency in acidic soils

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ALMT family proteins are plasma membrane-localized transporters that mediate malate efflux from root cells. 2. They are major determinants of OA exudation for Al detoxification, and may also contribute to P efficiency in acidic soils. 3. Some ALMT genes are rapidly activated by Al3+ or induced transcriptionally within hours.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene identification; transporter characterization; transgenic overexpression; OA exudation assay; stress phenotyping
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates malate exudation, enhancing Al tolerance and in some cases P acquisition.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Malate chelates Al3+ and mobilizes phosphate in acidic soils.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Al toxicity; phosphorus deficiency; acidic soils
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review emphasizes ALMT and MATE as the two predominant OA transporter families.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: multidrug and toxic compound extrusion / aluminum-activated citrate transporter family
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MATE/AACT family proteins are plasma membrane-localized transporters that mediate citrate efflux from roots. 2. They are major determinants of OA exudation for Al detoxification and likely contribute to P solubilization. 3. Their expression can be rapidly activated or transcriptionally induced under Al stress, depending on species.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene identification; transporter characterization; exudation measurements; transgenic studies
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates citrate exudation, improving Al tolerance and potentially P availability.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Citrate chelates Al3+ and mobilizes phosphate in acidic soils.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Al toxicity; phosphorus deficiency; acidic soils
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Low-P regulation of these transporters is noted as still insufficiently evaluated.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaALMT1 is identified as an Al-activated malate transporter involved in Al tolerance. 2. Overexpression of TaALMT1 in barley increased malate exudation and Al tolerance. 3. The same transgenic plants also showed increased P acquisition on acidic soils, supporting shared utility for Al tolerance and P efficiency.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression in barley; malate exudation assay; Al tolerance phenotyping; P acquisition assessment
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases malate exudation, improving Al tolerance and P acquisition on acidic soils.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Malate detoxifies Al and enhances phosphate availability.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Al toxicity; acidic soils; phosphorus deficiency
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong transgene candidate to improve barley adaptation to acidic soils.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmALMT1 was upregulated in the P-efficient soybean genotype HN89 under P deficiency. 2. Its induction was associated with increased malate release from roots. 3. GmALMT1 is also identified as a malate transporter involved in Al tolerance, supporting a link between P efficiency and Al tolerance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: expression analysis; root malate exudation measurement; genotype comparison
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with enhanced malate exudation under P deficiency and contributes to P efficiency/Al tolerance.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Malate chelates Al3+ and mobilizes phosphate.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contrasted with P-inefficient soybean genotypes BD2 and HN112 in the review.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In rice, Nrat1 mediates rapid uptake of Al3+ into root cells. 2. This internalized Al is then available for sequestration with OAs in vacuoles. 3. Nrat1 therefore contributes to internal Al detoxification linked to OA function.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes internal Al detoxification pathway by importing Al3+ into cells for subsequent OA-associated sequestration.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not an OA transporter itself, but central to the internal Al-OA detoxification pathway described in the review.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ALS1 is a tonoplast-localized transporter that sequesters Al3+ into vacuoles. 2. In vacuoles, Al forms complexes with OAs for internal detoxification. 3. ALS1 is thus an important component of the OA-associated internal Al detoxification mechanism.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances internal Al detoxification by vacuolar sequestration of Al for Al-OA complex formation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts downstream of Nrat1-mediated Al uptake in the model summarized by the review.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HvALMT1 overexpression in barley increased malate exudation. 2. This increase improved Al tolerance in transgenic barley. 3. It is presented as a successful transporter-engineering example for acidic soils.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression; exudation assay; Al tolerance phenotyping
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves Al tolerance through enhanced malate exudation.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HvAACT1 overexpression in barley increased citrate exudation. 2. This increased citrate efflux enhanced Al tolerance in transgenic barley. 3. HvAACT1 is highlighted as a successful engineering target for OA-mediated stress adaptation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic overexpression; citrate exudation assay; Al tolerance phenotyping
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves Al tolerance through enhanced citrate exudation.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbMATE expression is regulated by the AltSB locus in sorghum. 2. Introgression of AltSB into an Al-sensitive line generated Al-tolerant NILs. 3. This illustrates marker-assisted breeding potential for OA transporter-mediated Al tolerance.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhanced citrate exudation-associated Al tolerance in sorghum breeding materials.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Validated breeding target for improving Al tolerance via locus introgression.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review frames AltSB as regulating SbMATE expression.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses oxaloacetate with acetyl-CoA to form citrate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates Al3+ for detoxification and mobilizes phosphate in acidic soils.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CS is summarized as a key OA synthesis enzyme induced by excess Al3+ or low P. 2. Increased CS activity/expression can elevate citrate synthesis, OA exudation, Al tolerance and/or P efficiency. 3. Transgenic overexpression cases support its utility in engineering crops for acidic soils.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for OA synthesis; not conclusively established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced under excessive Al3+ or low P in adapted plants.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced under excessive Al3+ or low P in roots.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; metabolite profiling; OA exudation assay; growth/Al tolerance/P efficiency phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Al toxicity; phosphorus deficiency; acidic soils
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes citrate production/exudation, improving Al tolerance and/or P acquisition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Examples discussed include bacterial CS from Pseudomonas aeruginosa in tobacco/canola and DcCS from carrot in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful transgene candidate to increase citrate-mediated Al tolerance and P efficiency, but carbon metabolism disruption is a caution.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May disrupt carbon metabolism in some engineering attempts.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Organic acid biosynthesis / TCA-related malate metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelates Al3+ and contributes to phosphate mobilization/utilization.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MDH is highlighted as an OA metabolism enzyme whose increased activity can enhance OA synthesis. 2. Overexpression of MDH in alfalfa increased OA synthesis, P uptake, root elongation, biomass, and Al resistance. 3. MDH is therefore summarized as a promising engineering target for acidic soil adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for OA synthesis; not conclusively established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Can be induced under Al toxicity or low P.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Responsive to Al toxicity or low P depending on species/genotype.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; enzyme activity assay; metabolite profiling; root elongation and biomass phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Al toxicity; phosphorus deficiency; acidic soils
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances malate-related OA synthesis and improves Al tolerance and P efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review notes enzyme activity does not always correlate with OA exudation across species/genotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to enhance OA synthesis, especially combined with transporter engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible metabolic imbalance/disruption of carbon metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Organic acid biosynthesis / anaplerotic carbon fixation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Carboxylates phosphoenolpyruvate to generate oxaloacetate, supplying precursors for malate/citrate synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Organic acids detoxify Al3+ and improve phosphate availability/utilization.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PEPC is summarized as an Al/highly low-P responsive enzyme involved in OA synthesis. 2. Increased activity is associated with enhanced OA production under acidic-soil stresses. 3. The review presents PEPC as part of the core synthesis machinery but with less direct engineering evidence than CS/MDH.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Can be induced by excessive Al3+ or low P.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Stress responsive under high Al3+/low P conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: summarized enzyme activity analyses in prior studies
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports OA biosynthesis that contributes to Al tolerance and P efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented mainly as background synthesis enzyme with functional relevance to OA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential metabolic engineering target to raise OA precursor supply.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Malic enzyme involved in malate metabolism; the review associates it with elevated malate synthesis/exudation under Al stress.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Under Al stress, both transcript and protein abundance of SgME1 increased in an Al-tolerant and P-efficient stylo genotype. 2. This increase was accompanied by elevated malate synthesis and exudation. 3. SgME1 is thus implicated in adaptation of stylo to acidic soils.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by Al stress in an Al-tolerant, P-efficient genotype.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by Al stress in stylo roots.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene expression; protein accumulation; metabolite/exudation profiling under Al stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with increased malate synthesis/exudation and adaptation to acidic soils.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review states increased transcript and protein accumulation but does not describe transgenic validation here.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses oxaloacetate with acetyl-CoA to form citrate.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of DcCS in Arabidopsis increased P efficiency and improved growth under low P. 2. These effects were accompanied by increased plant P content and enhanced citrate exudation. 3. DcCS provides direct evidence that engineering citrate synthesis can improve low-P adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point; not established as sole rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous overexpression; P content measurement; citrate exudation assay; growth phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases citrate exudation and improves phosphorus efficiency under low P.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target for low-P tolerance on acidic soils.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formate oxidation in formate metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of VuFDH in tobacco increased tolerance to Al3+ and H+ stresses. 2. The effect was possibly due to reduced formate accumulation. 3. The review includes VuFDH as an example that modifying OA-related metabolism can aid adaptation to acidic soils.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves Al3+ and proton tolerance, possibly by reducing formate accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a canonical malate/citrate transporter/synthase, but included by the review as OA-metabolism-related engineering example.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: OA exudation / transport under Al stress
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Regulates Al3+-responsive ALMT and MATE gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ART1/STOP1 is summarized as the known regulator of Al3+-responsive ALMT and MATE genes in plants. 2. Through controlling these OA transporter genes, it influences OA exudation linked to Al tolerance. 3. Upstream Al signaling to ART1/STOP1 remains unknown.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of Al-induced organic acid exudation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Al-responsive OA exudation via regulation of transporter genes, contributing to Al tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned in conclusions/prospects as regulator of OA transporter genes; not functionally dissected in this review beyond summary.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Al3+ signaling (mechanism unresolved in the review)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Organic acids chelate Al3+ and improve adaptation to acidic soils.
